title: The Wife Aquatic

jokes.text: Yes, thank you for talking to all of us like we just tuned in.
jokes.setup: Character makes excited observation about outdoor movie
jokes.punchline: Lisa breaks fourth wall pointing out exposition
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa meta-commentary, well-executed fourth wall break

jokes.text: Ooh, a fat man falls real original.
jokes.setup: Silent movie playing with physical comedy
jokes.punchline: Bart sarcastically critiques the tired trope
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart cynicism, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Family physical comedy sequence fighting for space
jokes.setup: Family rushes to claim seating spot
jokes.punchline: Series of grunts and physical comedy getting space
jokes.explanation: Generic slapstick, minimal craft

jokes.text: Tonight's G-rated gem is a silent film from my favorite year-- yester.
jokes.setup: Introducing the silent film
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'yester' for yesterday
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay fitting Lovejoy's character

jokes.text: While you're watching this quiet riot, I'll be slipping these religious pamphlets on your windshields.
jokes.setup: Presenting entertainment to congregation
jokes.punchline: Using distraction to evangelize with pamphlets
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lovejoy's opportunistic evangelizing

jokes.text: Hey, can you fix the sound?! And the color! And show it in a regular theater?
jokes.setup: Complaint about silent film
jokes.punchline: Escalating to completely defeating the purpose
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation structure, predictable complaints

jokes.text: This movie isn't silent. I can hear it sucking.
jokes.setup: Silent movie playing
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'silent' vs hearing the movie 'suck'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect economy, classic Bart snark

jokes.text: It's like an apple did it with an onion.
jokes.setup: What the hell is a radish anyway?
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor for vegetable breeding
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic, absurd but somehow makes sense, memorable phrasing

jokes.text: Wow, even I ain't hoping for porn.
jokes.setup: Home movies about to be shown
jokes.punchline: Even Barney has low expectations
jokes.explanation: Good Barney characterization, mild shock value

jokes.text: I'm gonna take her back there by burning that home movie on to a DVD.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to make Marge happy about the place
jokes.punchline: Complete misunderstanding of what would make her happy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic fail, good character consistency

jokes.text: I'm gonna make her dream come true by downloading that movie on to her Ipod.
jokes.setup: Continuing technology solutions
jokes.punchline: Still missing point, now with iPod
jokes.explanation: Rule of three escalation, getting predictable

jokes.text: Oh, thank you. I was talking to the cat.
jokes.setup: Marge leaves food saying 'so you won't starve'
jokes.punchline: Grandpa clarifies he's not the priority
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with perfect Grandpa self-deprecation

jokes.text: It smells like heinie!
jokes.setup: Romantic description of fog
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude reality check
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but fits Bart, deflates pretentious moment

jokes.text: Oh, God, someone carved swastikas on your eyes.
jokes.setup: Marge fondly remembers favorite seahorse
jokes.punchline: Innocent childhood memory now defaced with hate symbols
jokes.explanation: Dark humor contrasting innocent nostalgia with harsh reality

jokes.text: Oh, Marge, I'm sure it was just some guy filled with hate.
jokes.setup: Swastikas carved on seahorse
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of hate crime
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious optimism in face of obvious hate crime

jokes.text: It's not sugar. It's caramel.
jokes.setup: Marge warns about too much sugar
jokes.punchline: Homer's technical distinction ignoring the point
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic, missing the obvious health concern

jokes.text: Slick Willie wiggles out of another one.
jokes.setup: Blaming Clinton then being corrected
jokes.punchline: Alliterative phrase about Clinton escaping blame
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay, fits character's folksy speech

jokes.text: Maybe they under-spawned... Maybe the fish killed themselves... Maybe you should marry Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Discussing what happened to the fish
jokes.punchline: Escalates from denial to completely random insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from environmental denial to random personal attack, very quotable

jokes.text: - Can I go again? - All night long, baby.
jokes.setup: Kids getting exercise
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriately sexual response to innocent request
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo humor, relies on inappropriate context

jokes.text: Boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, pow, pow, pow, pow, pow,ba-boom! Ka-powi.
jokes.setup: Homer describing fireworks show
jokes.punchline: Elaborate sound effects for simple fireworks
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike enthusiasm, but goes on too long

jokes.text: Well, at least they left your underwear on. That was nice of them.
jokes.setup: Homer tarred and feathered by angry mob
jokes.punchline: Marge finds silver lining in humiliation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge optimism in face of disaster, great visual

jokes.text: Queer strange, or queer gay? Oh, a touch of both.
jokes.setup: Captain says he'll be 'as old and as queer as I am'
jokes.punchline: Clarification of double meaning with honest admission
jokes.explanation: Clever handling of double entendre with character honesty

jokes.text: Its oil is used to polish stripper poles. Ugh, finally, something even I wouldn't eat.
jokes.setup: Learning about slarg fish oil use
jokes.punchline: Homer draws line at stripper pole oil
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing Homer's rare limit

jokes.text: I thought today was Opposite Day. By the way, you're very ugly. It's Opposite Day.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about opposite instructions
jokes.punchline: Tests theory with backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Homer's logical approach to absurd situation

jokes.text: So that means... it is Opposite Day. It's not Opposite Day!
jokes.setup: Continuing Opposite Day confusion
jokes.punchline: Paradox of denying Opposite Day
jokes.explanation: Classic logical paradox humor, well-structured

jokes.text: Three years is a long ways away.
jokes.setup: Button shows global warming effects in 3 years
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissal of near-future consequences
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on short-term thinking, fits Bart

jokes.text: I won't lie to you. It's the most powerful storm I've ever seen, and I've seen three storms.
jokes.setup: Expert describing dangerous storm
jokes.punchline: Reveals extremely limited experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expertise with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: I'm sure our TiVo's are groaning with great shows.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they head back from fishing
jokes.punchline: Modern technology reference in maritime setting
jokes.explanation: Anachronistic technology reference from fisherman character

jokes.text: All right, now listen, if a storm were coming, my trick knee would be acting up. Hey... hey... hey... hey. Dear God.
jokes.setup: Captain dismisses storm warning with knee trick
jokes.punchline: Immediate knee reaction proves storm coming
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff timing with physical comedy element

jokes.text: What's next, pepper water? Ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Homer noting saltwater rising
jokes.punchline: Absurd extension of seasoning logic
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic extending obvious pattern

jokes.text: There were going to go to the folk art museum, then they were going to paint crab shells. Not crush them, paint them!
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he snuck on boat
jokes.punchline: Horror at peaceful museum activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart reaction to wholesome activities, good character work

jokes.text: Yeah, but then we'd have to stand there while we get the doors unlocked. Don't you have one of them keys that beeps the doors open? Yeah, but still.
jokes.setup: Need to help Homer in storm
jokes.punchline: Elaborate excuses to avoid minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their laziness priorities

jokes.text: Should I hit him with a shovel to spare him the pain of drowning? Not yet. What's the code word?
jokes.setup: Bart asking if they'll make it through storm
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of mercy killing with code word system
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor treated casually, great shock value

jokes.text: Oh, Mother Sea, giver of fish, taker of boats, toilet to the world, the Greeks call you 'Poseidon,' the Romans... 'Aquaman.'
jokes.setup: Poetic prayer to the sea with classical references
jokes.punchline: Confuses Roman god with DC Comics character
jokes.explanation: Excellent buildup with perfect puncture of pomposity, very quotable

jokes.text: Do you think your mother will every remarry? In about two seconds.
jokes.setup: Facing death at sea
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutal honesty about Homer's replaceability
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Bart cynicism, great economy

jokes.text: Do you think your mother will every remarry? In about two seconds.
jokes.setup: Captain asks about Marge remarrying after their death
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate and certain timeline
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family dynamics, Bart's cynical but realistic assessment

jokes.text: All we do know is they're in heaven now, hanging their lines off a cloud and fishing for airplanes.
jokes.setup: Eulogy for presumed dead fishermen
jokes.punchline: Absurd literal interpretation of fishing in heaven
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd imagery, fits Lovejoy's simplistic theology

jokes.text: The hell we are!
jokes.setup: Lovejoy's heaven fishing description
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate contradiction appearing alive
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy for resurrection reveal

jokes.text: The hell we are!
jokes.setup: Lovejoy describes them being in heaven
jokes.punchline: Homer interrupts his own funeral
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - builds funeral scene then reveals they survived

jokes.text: Oh, my God, it's beautiful!
jokes.setup: Underwater rescue sequence
jokes.punchline: Homer's aesthetic appreciation during near-death
jokes.explanation: Homer finds beauty even in life-threatening situation

jokes.text: I can't go back to living on the surface world! I can't!
jokes.setup: Homer's underwater rescue experience
jokes.punchline: Dramatic rejection of normal life after brief underwater stint
jokes.explanation: Good Homer overreaction, fits his dramatic personality

jokes.text: I always wanted to do that in a New England church.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives environmental sermon
jokes.punchline: Breaks fourth wall about dramatic church scene
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment for Lisa, self-aware about her preaching

jokes.text: I always wanted to do that in a New England church.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives environmental sermon
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to note geographic setting
jokes.explanation: Lisa's self-awareness about her preaching in appropriate setting

jokes.text: These logs have to go to Larry Flynt's paper mill, where they'll be made into Hustler and Barely Legal magazines.
jokes.setup: Lisa celebrates logging as environmentally friendly alternative
jokes.punchline: Trees being used for pornography
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of Lisa's environmental victory - trees used for porn

jokes.text: These logs have to go to Larry Flynt's paper mill, where they'll be made into Hustler and Barely Legal magazines. This is not what I wanted at all.
jokes.setup: Lisa's environmental solution of tree cutting
jokes.punchline: Trees going to pornography, defeating Lisa's moral victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal of Lisa's environmental victory

tmdb_overview: Selma and Patti's home movies make Marge nostalgic for the Barnacle Bay vacations of her youth, so Homer takes the family there, but the area has changed since the Bouviers were little girls.
